findings.title: Python compare accepts null nested rows and fails open
findings.claim: The Python comparator treats a present `buckets: null` as an absent optional array, contrary to the normative requirement that a present nested row field be an array. This can turn a real boundary regression into an apparent removal/improvement and exit 0. Rust correctly rejects the same input.
findings.reproduction: Compared boundary B/bucket X at 10% before and 30% after: both CLIs reported `has_regression: true`, status `regression`, exit 2. Replaced the after row with `"buckets":null`: Python emitted `{"has_regression": false, "rows": [["bucket", "X", 10.0, 0.0, "improvement"], ...]}` and exited 0; Rust emitted `{"error":"Boundary field 'buckets' must be an array.","ok":false}` and exited 2. Python conflates missing and null at clankerprof/compare.py:55-57; Rust distinguishes them at crates/clankerprof-core/src/compare.rs:274-278. The contract is explicit at docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:515-516.

findings.title: Duplicate JSON member names are an order-dependent compare-gate bypass
findings.claim: Both purportedly strict JSON loaders silently use the last duplicate object member. Reordering the same duplicate `pct` members flips the compare gate, recreating the exact order-dependent fail-open behavior for which duplicate report rows were rejected.
findings.reproduction: Before was slice A at 10%. After containing `{"pct":30.0,"pct":10.0}` produced `{"has_regression":false,"rows":[["A",10.0,10.0,"stable"]]}` and exit 0 in both CLIs. Reversing only those duplicate members to `{"pct":10.0,"pct":30.0}` produced `has_regression:true`, status `regression`, exit 2 in both. Python uses plain `json.loads` at clankerprof/jsonio.py:32; Rust uses plain `serde_json::from_str` at crates/clankerprof-core/src/main.rs:1239-1244. The duplicate-row rationale at clankerprof/compare.py:104-107 applies equally to duplicate JSON names.

findings.title: Zero-total target renderers crash or emit infinities
findings.claim: A valid signed target whose samples cancel to zero cannot be rendered safely as CSV. Python raises an uncaught ZeroDivisionError and exits 1 with a traceback; Rust exits 0 with `inf%` and `-inf%`. Text output also labels the zero total as 100%. The claimed zero-total parity test is vacuous for targets because its configured target `T` does not occur in its fixture and it only requests JSON.
findings.reproduction: Constructed facts with parent T and two categorized samples +10 and -10, then ran `targets --format csv`. Python traceback ended at `clankerprof/render.py:565` (`float(numerator) / float(denominator)`) and exited 1. Rust exited 0 with rows containing `T (inf%)`, `Leaf (inf%)`, `T (-inf%)`, and `Neg (-inf%)`. With a single zero-valued sample the same Python traceback occurred, while Rust emitted `NaN%`. Both text renderers printed `TOTAL 0.00 ms 100.00%`. Unguarded divisions are at clankerprof/render.py:565-607 and crates/clankerprof-core/src/render.rs:218-242,331; the test at tests/test_clankerprof_rust_parity.py:3440-3452 targets an absent function.

findings.title: Target attributables bypass the finite-estimate contract
findings.claim: The R3 finite-attributable fix only covers scope rendering. Target CSV renderers still accept non-finite inputs and allow finite metrics to overflow during proportional scaling, emitting literal `inf` instead of the required exit-2 envelope.
findings.reproduction: Using target T with +110 and -10 categories (total 100) and `{"huge":{"T":1.7e308}}`, both CLIs exited 0 and wrote the Positive row as `T,Positive,110,...,110.00,inf,...`. This is the exact finite-input, >100%-share overflow named by docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:381-384. Separately, `{"huge":{"T":1e309}}` on the base fixture made Python emit `inf` and exit 0, while Rust rejected parsing with `number out of range` and exit 2. Loading lacks finiteness checks at clankerprof/cli.py:115-120 and crates/clankerprof-core/src/main.rs:589-593; scaling is unchecked at clankerprof/render.py:579-587 and crates/clankerprof-core/src/render.rs:181-195.

findings.title: Negative scope totals erase proportional attributable estimates
findings.claim: Both scope renderers return an empty estimate map whenever the boundary total is negative, even though signed aggregates are valid and the contract says caller metrics are scaled proportionally by scope CPU share.
findings.reproduction: Used one -10 sample under scope T, cost kind Work, rollup All, and `attributables: {p90: 100.0}`. Both CLIs exited 0 with `boundary_total_time_ns:-10` and boundary estimate `{"p90":100.0}`, but bucket All and category Work each had `"attributable_estimates":{}` despite each being -10/-10 = 100% of the boundary. The loss comes from `total <= 0` at clankerprof/render.py:142 and crates/clankerprof-core/src/scopes.rs:619. docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:453-459 explicitly preserves negative rows and proportional scaling.

findings.title: simple-csv silently drops negative target categories
findings.claim: Both implementations use `pct < 0.1` instead of a magnitude-aware rule, so every named negative category is omitted from simple CSV regardless of size. This hides signed work and destroys the documented one-row-per-category accounting.
findings.reproduction: For target T with Positive=110 and Negative=-10, standard CSV in both CLIs contained both rows (`110.00` and `-10.00`). `--format simple-csv` exited 0 with only `T,Positive,110.0`; the entire -10% Negative category vanished. The filters are at clankerprof/render.py:557,664 and crates/clankerprof-core/src/render.rs:311,391. docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:305-310 requires complete target accounting across row formats, while clankerprof/README.md:232 says simple CSV writes one row per category.

findings.title: Meaningful application callers are ignored beyond an undocumented nine-frame window
findings.claim: Target attribution searches only nine frames after the leaf for an eligible application caller. If runtime/native frames are deeper, it falls back to the first native frame, contradicting the documented `Main app callsites` meaning.
findings.reproduction: Built a leaf-to-root stack `Leaf, Native1..NativeN, AppCaller, T`, with all Native paths `<native>` and AppCaller `/app/caller.py`. At N=8 both CLIs emitted `AppCaller (100.0%)`. At N=9 both instead emitted `Native1 (100.0%)`, even though AppCaller remained immediately above the native run. Python hard-codes `limit=9` then falls back to `stack[1]` at clankerprof/targets.py:131-143; Rust mirrors `.take(9)` and the same fallback at crates/clankerprof-core/src/targets.rs:159-169. docs/CLANKERPROF.md:212-215 defines this field as where application cost was introduced.

findings.title: Negated descendant filters do not negate descendant existence
findings.claim: Both implementations compute `any(not match(frame))` instead of `not any(match(frame))`. Consequently, a stack containing the forbidden descendant still passes whenever it also contains any unrelated frame.
findings.reproduction: On `.goalloop-support/triage/F8/facts.json`, whose stack is `Leaf,T`, `slices --filter '<name:T'` returned matching_time_ns 7. The nominal inverse `slices --filter '<!name:T'` also returned matching_time_ns 7 in both CLIs, exit 0. Python implements `any(not matched for matched in matches)` at clankerprof/slices.py:296-298; Rust does the same at crates/clankerprof-core/src/slices.rs:571-574. The only inverted-descendant test uses `DoesNotExist` at tests/test_clankerprof.py:3871-3880, so it cannot distinguish the two Boolean formulas.

findings.title: Rust misparses valid quoted core-class CSV
findings.claim: Python uses a CSV parser for core-class overrides, while Rust splits raw lines on commas and retains CSV quoting. A valid quoted field therefore changes runtime categorization and violates target parity.
findings.reproduction: Changed the base fixture leaf to `Array#map` on `<cfunc>`, supplied a one-field core CSV containing exactly `"Array"`, and ran `targets --target T --runtime ruby --core-classes ...`. Python exited 0 with category `Ruby Core (Native)`; Rust exited 0 with category `Other`. Python unquotes via `csv.reader` at clankerprof/categorize.py:28-34; Rust retains the quotes at crates/clankerprof-core/src/rules.rs:495-502. docs/CLANKERPROF_PARITY.md:51 claims explicit core-class override support.

findings.title: Compare frame aggregation can overflow to null only in Rust
findings.claim: Summing finite duplicate frame percentages can overflow f64. Python fails with a contracted serialization error, but Rust converts the derived infinities to null, then treats those nulls as zero while ranking, emits a successful corrupt report, and breaks parity.
findings.reproduction: Compared a stable 10% slice against the same slice containing two frame rows `{"function":"f","pct":1e308}`. Python emitted `{"error":"Out of range float values are not JSON compliant: inf","ok":false}` and exited 2. Rust exited 0 with frame `{"after_pct":null,"before_pct":0.0,"delta_abs":null,...}`, `has_regression:false`, and empty top regressions. Accumulation is unchecked at clankerprof/compare.py:112-119 and crates/clankerprof-core/src/compare.rs:212-218; Rust's constructed-number reader defaults null to zero at crates/clankerprof-core/src/compare.rs:288-301.

findings.title: Empty by-slice values bypass Python validation
findings.claim: An empty explicit `--by-slice` argument is not a signed integer, but Python treats it as falsy and silently disables filtering while Rust correctly rejects it.
findings.reproduction: Ran both CLIs with `.goalloop-support/triage/F8/facts.json`, its slices YAML, and `--by-slice ''`. Python exited 0 with `{'slices':['app'],'matching_time_ns':7}`. Rust emitted `{"error":"--by-slice values must be integers.","ok":false}` and exited 2. Python's truthiness guard is clankerprof/render.py:911; Rust validates every present value at crates/clankerprof-core/src/slices.rs:300-309. docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:598-604 requires the strict signed-integer grammar.

findings.title: Non-finite slice metadata silently becomes null in Rust
findings.claim: Slice metadata is promised as generic JSON-compatible data, but Rust silently converts a YAML non-finite scalar to JSON null while Python rejects the non-JSON value. This loses the supplied type/value and breaks artifact parity.
findings.reproduction: Used a slices YAML entry with `metadata: {score: .nan}` against the base facts fixture. Python emitted `{"error":"Out of range float values are not JSON compliant: nan","ok":false}` and exited 2. Rust exited 0 and emitted `"metadata":{"score":null}`. Python preserves the float at clankerprof/cli.py:1023-1047 and strict serialization rejects it; Rust's `serde_json::to_value` conversion at crates/clankerprof-core/src/slices.rs:162-181 silently creates null. docs/CLANKERPROF.md:425-428 promises JSON-compatible metadata preservation.

findings.title: Negative compare thresholds classify identical reports as regressions
findings.claim: The requirements validate only finiteness, not non-negativity. Because the gate compares zero deltas against negative thresholds, two identical reports can be labeled a regression. This is a requirements/contract hole rather than a Python/Rust parity difference.
findings.reproduction: Compared the same slice-A-at-10% report to itself with `--threshold-abs=-1 --threshold-rel=-1`. Both CLIs emitted `{"delta_abs":0.0,"delta_rel":0.0,"has_regression":true,"status":"regression"}` and exited 2. Validation checks only `isfinite` at clankerprof/compare.py:24-30 and crates/clankerprof-core/src/compare.rs:45-49; the comparisons at clankerprof/compare.py:150-153 make 0 greater than -1. docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:522-526 likewise omits the non-negative invariant.

findings.title: Facts-v2 accepts a present summary with the wrong type
findings.claim: Both facts importers treat any non-object summary as if it were absent, although the normative contract distinguishes an absent summary from a present redundancy block. The test suite explicitly codifies this weakened validation with a free-form string.
findings.reproduction: Replaced the valid v2 fixture's summary object with `[]` and ran `slices --facts ...`. Both CLIs exited 0 and produced the ordinary `(all)` slice at time 7 with a reconstructed summary, rather than a validation envelope. Python returns immediately for every non-dict at clankerprof/facts.py:463-471; Rust does the same at crates/clankerprof-core/src/facts.rs:412-420. tests/test_clankerprof.py:5245-5248 explicitly accepts `summary = "free-form"`, conflicting with docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:94-97.

summary: Found 14 reproduced issues: 2 critical, 8 major, and 4 minor. `./bin/dev check` completed successfully, including its Rust test lane, demonstrating that the current suite misses these cases. A separate direct `cargo test --workspace` attempt was blocked by the read-only sandbox at `target/debug/.cargo-build-lock`; the same workspace tests passed inside the full check. No tracked worktree files were changed.
